summary:
The briefing is compiled. ⚠️ One note: the email did **not** send — the mail system returned an error (`GMAIL_USER`/`GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD` are not configured as secrets). I've saved the full briefing to disk as a fallback so nothing is lost, but **the credentials must be set before the founder will receive this in their inbox.**

Here is the complete briefing content:

---

## Guided Childhood — Morning Briefing
**Saturday 7 June 2026**

### Executive Summary
The single most important fact today is that **England's statutory phone-free school law lands on 29 June** — giving us a hard, three-week window to pitch schools a curriculum that fills the gap phones leave behind, before term ends. On the £1M ARR mission we are at the literal start line: **£0 ARR, 0 schools, 0 parents and — the real emergency — a £0 pipeline**, so nothing is yet in motion that could become revenue. **The one action right now:** personally send direct outreach to 10 named DSLs/MAT leads and lock at least 3 discovery calls this week, leading with Navigator's KCSIE-2026 message #1 (the most time-pressured, highest-intent hook). ⚠️ No agent reported a failure, but every one independently raised the same blocking question — we still need confirmation that Guided Childhood produces a date-stamped, exportable filtering/monitoring review log AND that our age-assurance meets Ofcom's "highly effective" standard; both underpin live promises and must be answered before outreach goes out.

---

### 🔭 Scout — Intelligence
1. **Phone-free becomes statutory law from 29 June 2026** — DfE expects all schools "phone-free by default." *(Commons Library CBP-10241)*
2. **Ofcom's statutory age-assurance effectiveness report due end of July 2026** — the verification bar is rising; our 4–16 onboarding must be demonstrably robust.
3. **"Growing up in the online world" consultation closed 26 May 2026** — proposes age restrictions on social media, gaming, AI chatbots and screen-time limits.
4. **Ofcom fined Kick £800,000 for age-check failures** and is escalating 2026/27 enforcement onto AI moderation, deepfakes and child protection.
5. **Internet Matters' "Are children safer online?" (30 Apr 2026) + "Children's Wellbeing in a Digital World Index 2026"** — fresh four-year trend data to ground our marketing.

**Action:** Pull the Internet Matters report and DfE guidance; brief Sage to map features against the ban so Herald can pitch before term ends.
**Open question:** Does our age-assurance meet Ofcom's "highly effective" standard — and if not, what's the gap?

---

### 🛡️ Guardian — Safeguarding
KCSIE 2026 (published 1 Sept 2026): (1) reframes intimate-image abuse to include AI deepfakes; (2) tightens filtering/monitoring — at least annual review across all devices with a kept record. DSLs are in the prep window now.

**Action:** Draft a one-page "KCSIE 2026 readiness" summary for Navigator.
**Open question:** Do we generate a date-stamped, exportable filtering/monitoring record for Ofsted — and if not, can we build it before September?

---

### 🧭 Navigator — Sales
1. MAT Director of Safeguarding — KCSIE deepfake + filtering-record hook *(send first)*
2. Headteacher, large secondary — 29 June phone-free duty
3. Deputy Head / DSL (KS2–KS5) — Internet Matters deepfake data
4. PSHE/RSHE Lead — Sept RSHE update
5. MAT CEO — Ofcom benchmark + £800k fine

**Action:** Send message #1 now.
**Open question:** Do we have an exportable filtering/monitoring log a DSL could show Ofsted?

---

### ✍️ Sage — Content
LinkedIn piece framing the phone-free ban as an opportunity to teach digital literacy; cites that only 31% of children believe enough is being done to keep them safe online.

**Action:** Post before 10am; send Herald the feature-to-ban mapping.
**Open question:** Does our age-assurance meet Ofcom's standard — which step is the gap?

---

### 📣 Herald — PR
Live Sourcee #journorequest "Child Safety Experts on AI Chatbots & Cyber Intimacy Impact" — a journalist is seeking our founder by description. Pitch offers a same-day quote on the "danger the phone ban can't reach" home-environment angle.

**Action:** Respond to the #journorequest today.
**Open question:** Can the founder confirm two on-record, parent-facing soundbites on AI-chatbot "cyber intimacy" risk?

---

### 💷 Compass — Revenue
ARR £0 / Target £1,000,000 / Gap £1,000,000 / 0.0% complete / 0 schools / 0 parents / **£0 pipeline**. The £0 pipeline is the real emergency.

**The ONE action:** Send outreach to 10 named DSLs/MAT ops leads, lock 3 discovery calls this week.
**Open question:** What is the target annual price of a single school/MAT licence?

---

### ⚡ Founder's To-Do (priority order)
1. Answer the two blocking questions (filtering log + age-assurance standard).
2. Approve & send Navigator's message #1 now.
3. Send 10 DSL/MAT outreach messages, lock 3 discovery calls.
4. Post Sage's LinkedIn piece before 10am.
5. Reply to the Sourcee #journorequest.
6. Confirm a target licence price.

---

**Next step for delivery:** Set `GMAIL_USER` and `GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD` as secrets and I'll re-send this to justin@thesocialbillboard.com immediately.

Scout.output:
I have enough verified, dated material. Compiling the briefing.

---

**1. Mobile phones become statutory phone-free law in England from 29 June 2026 — DfE guidance now expects all schools "phone-free by default."**
Why it matters: Schools will need replacement tools for digital-life skills inside the building — Guided Childhood can position as the curriculum-aligned answer when phones are out of pupils' hands.
Source: https://commonslibrary.parliament.uk/research-briefings/cbp-10241/

**2. Ofcom must publish its statutory age assurance effectiveness report by end of July 2026 — and is already telling platforms to "do much, much more to know the age of children."**
Why it matters: The age-verification bar is rising fast; Guided Childhood's onboarding for 4–16s must be demonstrably robust before this report sets the new compliance benchmark.
Source: https://www.ofcom.org.uk/online-safety/illegal-and-harmful-content/roadmap-to-regulation

**3. Government's "Growing up in the online world" consultation closed 26 May 2026 — proposals include age restrictions on social media, gaming and AI chatbots, plus screen-time limits.**
Why it matters: Signals where regulation is heading on AI chatbots and addictive design — Guided Childhood should pre-empt these in product design and respond publicly to shape its market position.
Source: https://www.gov.uk/government/consultations/growing-up-in-the-online-world-a-national-consultation/growing-up-in-the-online-world-a-national-conversation

**4. Ofcom fined Kick Online Entertainment £800,000 for age-check failures and is escalating its 2026/27 enforcement onto AI moderation, deepfakes and child protection (enforcement tracker updated 3 June 2026).**
Why it matters: Active fines prove the regulator will pursue non-compliance — a credible, child-safe platform is now a differentiator parents and schools will actively seek.
Source: https://www.ofcom.org.uk/online-safety/protecting-children/Ofcom-fines-porn-company-800k-for-failing-to-introduce-age-checks

**5. Internet Matters' "Online Safety Act: Are children safer online?" (30 April 2026) and "Children's Wellbeing in a Digital World Index 2026" provide fresh four-year trend data on children's online lives.**
Why it matters: Independent, citable evidence of where children remain unprotected — use it to ground Guided Childhood's marketing claims and target the specific harms the data exposes.
Source: https://www.internetmatters.org/hub/research/online-safety-act-report-2026/

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Pull the Internet Matters "Are children safer online?" report and the DfE phone-free guidance, then brief Sage to map Guided Childhood's features against the 29 June statutory phone ban so Herald can pitch schools before term ends.
YOUR QUESTION: Does Guided Childhood's current age-assurance method meet the "highly effective" standard Ofcom will benchmark in its July 2026 report — and if not, what's our gap?

Guardian.output:
I have a clear picture. The most significant, timely, and directly relevant development for Guided Childhood as of 2026-06-07 is the **KCSIE 2026 final guidance** (to be published 1 September 2026) — specifically its new explicit duties around **AI-generated intimate images/deepfakes** and tightened **filtering and monitoring** expectations. This is the development DSLs are actively preparing for right now, in the window between consultation closing (22 April) and September implementation.

---

**KEY SAFEGUARDING DEVELOPMENT**

The Department for Education's **Keeping Children Safe in Education (KCSIE) 2026** statutory guidance — confirmed for publication on **1 September 2026** — introduces two changes that sit squarely in Guided Childhood's lane. First, it explicitly reframes intimate-image abuse to include **"non-consensual self-generated intimate images and/or videos including those generated using AI, e.g. deepfakes,"** placing AI-enabled child-on-child abuse on the safeguarding agenda for the first time. Second, it **tightens filtering and monitoring duties**, expecting schools to review arrangements *at least once per academic year*, across all devices and locations, and to **keep a record of those checks**.
Source (DfE draft, consultation document): https://consult.education.gov.uk/independent-education-and-school-safeguarding-division/keeping-children-safe-in-education-2026-revisions/supporting_documents/keeping_children_safe_in_education_2026_draft_for_consultationpdf-1

**HOW GUIDED CHILDHOOD SHOULD RESPOND**

Position Guided Childhood as the tool that helps DSLs evidence the *new* KCSIE 2026 expectations before September: offer a simple, recorded annual review of filtering/monitoring across devices, plus age-appropriate digital-literacy content that helps children recognise and report AI-generated/deepfake intimate-image abuse. Lead with the genuine child-welfare benefit — children understanding the risk and feeling safe to disclose — not the compliance burden, so schools see protection first and paperwork second. This gives DSLs a ready-made, auditable line in their safeguarding records ahead of inspection.

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Draft a one-page "KCSIE 2026 readiness" summary for Navigator that maps Guided Childhood's filtering-review log and deepfake-awareness lessons directly against the two new KCSIE clauses, so it can be quoted verbatim in DSL outreach.
YOUR QUESTION: Does Guided Childhood currently generate a date-stamped, exportable record of filtering/monitoring reviews that a DSL could show an Ofsted inspector — and if not, can we build that before September?

Navigator.output:
I have enough verified, dated material to build five strong, current outreach targets. Here is today's briefing.

---

## Guided Childhood — Five LinkedIn Outreach Targets, 2026-06-07

**1. MAT Director of Safeguarding (cross-phase, ~12-school trust, England)**
- **(a) Role/type:** MAT-level Designated Safeguarding Lead / Director of Safeguarding at a medium multi-academy trust covering KS2–KS5.
- **(b) Why today:** Guardian flagged that KCSIE 2026 (published 1 Sept) explicitly reframes intimate-image abuse to include AI deepfakes *and* tightens filtering/monitoring to a recorded annual review across all devices. A MAT safeguarding director has to roll this out across every school before September — they're in the prep window right now (consultation closed 22 April). This is the single most relevant person for our value lead.
- **(c) Message (291 chars):**
> "Hi [Name] — I imagine KCSIE 2026's new deepfake clause and the annual filtering-review record are already on your September prep list across the trust. We help DSLs evidence both with date-stamped logs and pupil deepfake-awareness lessons. Would a quick share of what we built be useful?"
- **(d) Hook used:** Guardian — "KCSIE 2026… explicitly reframes intimate-image abuse to include 'non-consensual self-generated intimate images… including those generated using AI, e.g. deepfakes'… tightens filtering and monitoring duties, expecting schools to review arrangements at least once per academic year… and to keep a record of those checks."

---

**2. Headteacher, large secondary (1,200+ pupils, England)**
- **(a) Role/type:** Headteacher of a state secondary, the setting most affected by phone policy.
- **(b) Why today:** Scout flagged that phone-free becomes statutory law from 29 June 2026, with DfE expecting schools "phone-free by default." Heads need replacement tools for digital-life skills *inside the building* once phones are out — and they need it before this term ends. Timely, specific, term-deadline relevance.
- **(c) Message (286 chars):**
> "Hi [Name] — with the 29 June phone-free duty landing mid-term, lots of heads are asking how pupils still build digital-life skills once phones are out of the building. We've mapped our curriculum content to exactly that gap. Happy to send the one-pager — no pitch, just useful?"
- **(d) Hook used:** Scout #1 — "Mobile phones become statutory phone-free law in England from 29 June 2026 — DfE guidance now expects all schools 'phone-free by default.'"

---

**3. Deputy Head / DSL, primary or all-through school (KS2–KS5 oversight)**
- **(a) Role/type:** Deputy Headteacher who also holds the DSL role across multiple sites — a profile pattern confirmed in today's search (e.g. a Deputy Head responsible for three sites KS2–KS5).
- **(b) Why today:** Guardian's KCSIE 2026 deepfake duty + Internet Matters' deepfake evidence (Scout #5) gives a child-welfare-first hook. A site-spanning DSL has to make AI/deepfake risk age-appropriate from upper primary up — and Internet Matters' data shows nude-deepfake content rose 400%+, so this is a real, evidenced harm, not a scare tactic.
- **(c) Message (294 chars):**
> "Hi [Name] — managing the DSL role across KS2–KS5 sites, you'll have seen Internet Matters' data on how fast nude-deepfake harm is hitting younger children. We've built age-appropriate lessons so pupils can recognise and report it — and feel safe to. Would the resource help your team?"
- **(d) Hook used:** Scout #5 — Internet Matters' "Children's Wellbeing in a Digital World" / deepfake trend data ("Independent, citable evidence of where children remain unprotected — use it to… target the specific harms the data exposes").

---

**4. PSHE / RSHE Lead, secondary school (England)**
- **(a) Role/type:** PSHE/RSHE curriculum lead — the person rebuilding the curriculum against the 2026 statutory RSHE update (deepfakes, image-sharing, screen time now named areas).
- **(b) Why today:** PSHE leads must update curriculum by September 2026, and the new RSHE areas (deepfakes, online misogyny, screen-time/mental-health) map directly to our content. Scout #3 (the "Growing up in the online world" consultation on AI chatbots and screen-time limits) signals the direction of travel — a forward-looking PSHE lead wants content that's already ahead of it.
- **(c) Message (293 chars):**
> "Hi [Name] — with the Sept RSHE update naming deepfakes and screen-time, and the 'Growing up in the online world' consultation pointing the same way on AI chatbots, you're effectively rebuilding the online-safety strand. We've sequenced lessons to that. Glad to share, if it saves you time?"
- **(d) Hook used:** Scout #3 — "Government's 'Growing up in the online world' consultation closed 26 May 2026 — proposals include age restrictions on social media, gaming and AI chatbots, plus screen-time limits."

---

**5. MAT CEO (trust-wide strategic lead, England)**
- **(a) Role/type:** Chief Executive of a multi-academy trust — sets trust-wide procurement and risk strategy.
- **(b) Why today:** Scout #2 and #4 show the regulatory bar rising fast — Ofcom's statutory age-assurance report is due end of July 2026, and it fined Kick £800k for age-check failures. A MAT CEO choosing whole-trust digital tools wants providers that are demonstrably child-safe *before* that benchmark lands, and a credible platform is now a parent/governor reassurance point.
- **(c) Message (296 chars):**
> "Hi [Name] — with Ofcom's age-assurance benchmark due end of July and £800k fines already issued, MAT leaders are rightly asking whether trust-wide tools meet the rising child-safety bar. We're built for 4–16s with that in mind. Would a short note on our approach be helpful for your team?"
- **(d) Hook used:** Scout #2 — "Ofcom must publish its statutory age assurance effectiveness report by end of July 2026" (reinforced by Scout #4, the £800k Kick fine).

---

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Send message #1 to the MAT Director of Safeguarding now, because the KCSIE 2026 September deadline makes the deepfake + filtering-record hook the most time-pressured and highest-intent of the five.

YOUR QUESTION: Does Guided Childhood currently generate a date-stamped, exportable filtering/monitoring review log a DSL could show Ofsted — because messages #1 and #3 promise exactly that, and I need to confirm it's real before I send.

Sage.output:
News hook used: Mobile phones become statutory phone-free law in England from 29 June 2026 — DfE guidance now expects all schools to be "phone-free by default," including breaks and lunchtimes (Commons Library, CBP-10241).

In three weeks, the phones go away for good.

From 29 June, England's schools become phone-free by default — not just lessons, but breaks and lunchtimes too. For many of you, this simply makes statutory what your corridors already practise. It's a welcome line in the sand.

But here's the quiet truth I keep coming back to: taking the phone out of a child's hand doesn't take the online world out of their head. A Year 8 still goes home to group chats, gaming lobbies and AI chatbots that don't recognise a school gate. And the latest Internet Matters research is sobering — only 31% of children believe enough is being done to keep them safe online.

So the ban isn't the finish line. It's an opportunity.

The schools getting this right are using the calmer, phone-free school day to *teach* the skills children will need the moment they switch the screen back on after 3:15 — how to spot a manipulated image, when a chatbot is steering them, why a private account matters. Digital literacy doesn't compete with the ban; it completes it.

One practical takeaway: before term ends, map your phone policy against your PSHE and online-safety curriculum. Where the phone disappears, the lesson should appear. They are two halves of the same safeguarding promise.

How is your school planning to use the phone-free day? I'd genuinely like to hear what's working.

#Safeguarding #DigitalLiteracy #OnlineSafety #Headteachers #EdTech

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Post this LinkedIn piece before 10am for best DSL and Headteacher reach, then send Herald the feature-to-phone-ban mapping so he can pitch schools before term ends.
YOUR QUESTION: Does Guided Childhood's current age-assurance method meet Ofcom's "highly effective" standard ahead of the July 2026 benchmark report — and if not, exactly which step is the gap?

Herald.output:
I have a strong, live PR opportunity. Here's my brief.

---

**Scout finding used:** Finding #3 — Government's "Growing up in the online world" consultation (closed 26 May 2026), with its proposals on **age restrictions for AI chatbots** — combined with the live, citable hook of a journalist actively requesting child-safety experts on AI chatbots *right now*.

---

**Top PR opportunity of the day:**
A live #journorequest on Sourcee — *"Child Safety Experts on AI Chatbots & Cyber Intimacy Impact"* — is seeking child/online-safety experts to comment on teenage boys' use of AI chatbots (sourcee.app/journo-request/child-safety-experts-on-ai-chatbots-and-cyber-intimacy-impact). This is the single best hook today because it (a) is a journalist *asking* for our founder by description, and (b) rides a fast-moving news wave: Florida's lawsuit against OpenAI over child safety (NYT, 1 June), the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Act bringing AI chatbots under the Online Safety Act (Royal Assent, Feb 2026), and Internet Matters' finding that **two-thirds of 9–17 year-olds now use AI chatbots**. This is the *next best angle* to Sage's LinkedIn post — Sage owns the school phone-ban/digital-literacy angle; this pitch owns the **AI chatbot / home-environment / "the danger the phone ban can't reach"** angle, which Sage's post explicitly named but did not develop.

**3-sentence pitch (send today):**
> Two-thirds of UK 9–17s now use AI chatbots, yet most parents have no idea their teen is forming a relationship with one — and as England's statutory school phone-ban lands on 29 June, the harm simply moves home, where no school gate can stop it. Guided Childhood's founder can speak precisely to the "cyber intimacy" angle you're exploring: how chatbots manipulate emotionally vulnerable teenage boys, why current age-assurance fails them, and the three questions every parent should ask tonight. Happy to provide a same-day quote, data, or a 15-minute call — fully briefed on the Internet Matters evidence and the new Online Safety Act chatbot duties.

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Respond to the Sourcee #journorequest "Child Safety Experts on AI Chatbots & Cyber Intimacy Impact" with the pitch above, offering the founder for a same-day quote or call.
YOUR QUESTION: Can the founder confirm two on-record, parent-facing soundbites on AI-chatbot "cyber intimacy" risk to teenage boys, so I can lead future pitches with a ready quote rather than just an offer?

# REVENUE SNAPSHOT — Guided Childhood
*As of 2026-06-07 (data last updated 2026-06-04)*

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Current ARR** | £0 |
| **Target ARR** | £1,000,000 |
| **Gap to £1M** | **£1,000,000** |
| **% Complete** | **0.0%** |
| **Schools signed** | 0 |
| **Parent subscribers** | 0 |
| **Pipeline value** | £0 |

## The honest read

You are at the start line. Pre-revenue, zero schools, zero parents, and — most damning — **an empty pipeline**. A £0 ARR is recoverable; a £0 pipeline is the real emergency, because it means there is currently nothing in motion that *could* become revenue. ARR is a lagging indicator; pipeline is the leading one, and right now both read zero.

There is no conversion problem to optimise, no cohort to analyse, no per-school economic to model — because nothing has entered the funnel. The entire mission hinges on getting the *first* qualified school conversation booked. You cannot improve a number that doesn't exist yet; you can only start it.

## The ONE action

Today, personally book **3 discovery calls with named safeguarding leads (DSLs) or MAT operations directors** for this week — direct outreach, not marketing — because the only way to move a £0 pipeline is to put real B2B conversations into it, and one signed school licence is worth far more ARR per unit of effort than any parent subscription.

YOUR ACTION TODAY: Send personalised outreach to 10 named DSLs/MAT ops leads and lock at least 3 discovery calls into the calendar this week.
YOUR QUESTION: What is the target annual price of a single school/MAT safeguarding licence, so I can tell you exactly how many schools you must sign to hit £1M and pace the pipeline accordingly?
